obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Page background, card surfaces, primary canvas — the default stage for all content

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Primary text, pill button fills, display headlines on dark, thin borders for reverse-contrast components

carbon.$description: Carbon — Input fields, secondary surface elevation, subtle dark borders — one shade above Obsidian for depth without contrast

graphite.$description: Graphite — Elevated surface layer above the canvas — for overlay cards, tooltips, and floating panels

smoke.$description: Smoke — Headline accent borders, secondary text, low-emphasis dividers

ash.$description: Ash — Body-level borders, inactive dividers, low-contrast separators between sections

near-black.$description: Near Black — Subtle hairlines and body borders that are nearly invisible against Obsidian

arial.$description: Arial — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

raveo-variable-variable.$description: Display and heading font at its heaviest weight (1000). The variable axis is not used — the system commits to maximum weight to create typographic mass without chromatic weight. Sizes scale from 64px display down to 12px micro-labels (e.g., nav items 'NEW', 'Feed'). Letter-spacing tightens aggressively at large sizes (-0.04em at 64px, -0.02em at 22px) to lock the glyphs into a single visual block. Font features 'blwf', 'cv03', 'cv04', 'cv09', 'cv11' activate specific stylistic alternates — a signature typographic fingerprint. No web-safe substitute captures this; 'Inter' at weight 900 is the closest match, but weight and openness will differ.

system-sans-serif.$description: Secondary utility text — nav links, metadata, timestamps, captions. Weight 400 only; no bold variants. The system font acts as a quiet complement to Raveo's display voice.

inter-display.$description: One-off paragraph text at 22px — lighter weight than Raveo at the same size creates a body/heading tension within the same line height

obsidian.$description: Surface level 0: Page canvas — the base floor for all content

carbon.$description: Surface level 1: Input fields and subtle elevation — one shade above the canvas for form surfaces

graphite.$description: Surface level 2: Floating panels, overlay cards, tooltips — two shades above the canvas

paper-white.$description: Surface level 3: Inverted surface for filled pill buttons and the rare reverse-contrast element
